comment: Render produces the pure parts: ai-plugin.json is rendered in full (URL-derived namespace + auth reference_id via the mcpNamespace/mcpAuthRef expr functions + the unconditional dynamic-discovery RemoteMCPServer runtime), and m365agents.yml is the v1.12 skeleton WITHOUT the auth step. The auth wiring is post-render injection, NOT render, for two reasons the PR makes explicit: (1) it is impure — probed oauth authorization/token URLs, DCR well-known auto-discovery that falls back to a placeholder + warning, and a v1.12 -> v1.13 schema bump on dcr/register; (2) it must run through the SAME code path as the `add` flow (mcpAuthScaffolder.injectMCPAuthActionToYml + ActionInjector + persistMCPAuthCredentialEnvVars) so create and add never drift. The step derives namespace / registration id from mcpServerUrl itself (deriveMCPServerNameFromUrl -> namespace `apigithubc`, id `MCP_DA_AUTH_ID_APIGITHUBC`, never `action_1`); passing them in would duplicate that derivation. Routed only when DT is on, so credential persistence to env files (includeCredentialRefs) is unconditional. See proposal §13.1.

steps.comment: Guards the create contract: the target dir has no colliding files so render writes every file. Possibly redundant if the engine already guarantees create scaffolds into a fresh empty dir — pending alignment with the v4 engine; not from PR #16067.
